build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: Each floor is a procedurally generated platformer level with an exit at the bottom. The layout changes every run — platforms, traps, and enemy placement are randomized. Score 0 if levels are static. Score 0.5 at most if some randomness exists but overall structure is always the same. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

The explorer has limited tools: ropes (create climbable lines upward) and bombs (destroy terrain). Both are consumable and scarce. Using them is a meaningful resource decision. Score 0 if no consumable tools exist. Score 0.5 at most if tools exist but are unlimited or have no terrain interaction. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Physics objects (pots, crates, skulls) can be picked up and thrown at enemies or traps. Objects interact with the environment — thrown pots trigger arrow traps, crates break on impact. Score 0 if no throwable objects exist. Score 0.5 at most if objects exist but have no physics interaction. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

A ghost timer activates after lingering too long on a floor (approximately 3 minutes), spawning an invincible pursuer that forces the player to move forward. The ghost is visible and threatening. Score 0 if no time pressure exists. Score 0.5 at most if a timer exists but has no consequence. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

Death is permanent — dying sends the player back to floor 1 with no carried resources. Health persists across floors within a run. The run ends on a game-over screen with stats. Score 0 if death has no consequence or health resets between floors. Score 0.5 at most if permadeath exists but no stats are shown. Score 1 requires the mechanic to be presented in a visually authored context with real sprites or illustrated assets. Score 0.5 at most if the mechanic works but is represented entirely by programmatic shapes, solid-color fills, or default Godot widgets.

At least 4 distinct trap types exist (arrow traps, spike pits, crush blocks, dart blowers) each with different trigger conditions and avoidance strategies. Traps interact with physics objects. Score 0 if no traps exist. Score 0.5 at most if traps exist but fewer than 3 types are present. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

Shops appear every few floors with items for sale (health, tools, weapons, accessories). A shopkeeper NPC is present. Stealing triggers shopkeeper aggression for the rest of the run. Score 0 if no shops exist. Score 0.5 at most if shops exist but have no shopkeeper or stealing mechanic. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 3 distinct environment themes change every few floors (caves, jungle, ice, temple) with different enemy types, trap configurations, and visual styles per theme. Score 0 if the environment never changes. Score 0.5 at most if themes change but enemies and traps remain identical. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

At least 5 distinct enemy types with different behaviors (patrollers, jumpers, flyers, spitters, exploders). Enemies interact with traps and physics objects. Score 0 if fewer than 3 enemy types exist. Score 0.5 at most if enemies exist but all behave identically. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A game-over screen shows depth reached, gold collected, enemies defeated, and cause of death. A shortcut system unlocks after meeting conditions, allowing experienced players to start at later floors. Score 0 if no results or shortcuts exist. Score 0.5 at most if results show but no shortcut system exists. Score 1 requires content variants to be visually distinct with real authored art (different sprites, illustrated elements). Score 0.5 at most if variants exist but are represented by programmatic shapes, color swaps, or text labels without real asset differences.

A styled title screen shows the cave entrance with depth markers and the game name. Score 0 if the game starts directly in gameplay or uses raw default UI. Score 0.5 at most if a title exists but is sparse. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The game flow connects title -> descent -> floors -> shops -> game over -> retry without restarting. The ghost timer and floor transitions are smooth. Score 0 if transitions are missing. Score 0.5 at most if flow exists but is broken. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

During gameplay, health, gold, rope count, bomb count, and current floor are visible in a clear HUD. The ghost timer warning is visible when approaching the limit. Score 0 if no HUD exists. Score 0.5 at most if HUD is incomplete. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

Readability at 1280x720 is strong: the explorer, enemies, traps, physics objects, terrain, and collectibles are all clearly distinguishable. Score 0 if elements blend together. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are dominated by raw shapes. Score 1 requires the information to be presented through themed, styled UI with real assets. Score 0.5 at most if readability relies on plain text, unstyled labels, or raw geometric shapes.

The art direction is coherent across title, floors, enemies, traps, shops, and UI — a consistent spelunking adventure aesthetic with earthy palette. Score 0 if mostly default UI or mismatched placeholders dominate. Score 0.5 at most if visuals are sparse or generic. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Cave environments use real sprite or tile art for terrain, traps, enemies, and the explorer — not plain ColorRect fills. Different themes have distinct tilesets. Score 0 if environments are uniform color fills. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

HUD and UI chrome: health display, item counters, shop interface, and game-over screen use styled themed widgets — not raw unstyled Godot defaults. Score 0 if primary UI elements are obviously unstyled. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.

Animation and feedback: the explorer has run/jump/whip animations, enemies have movement animations, traps trigger with visible wind-up, bombs explode with particles, and the ghost has a menacing entrance animation. Score 0 if all state changes snap silently. Score 0.5 at most if animation is minimal. Score 0.5 at most if the game uses programmatic shapes (ColorRect, solid fills, or untextured geometry) instead of real illustrated or pixel-art assets.
